attacking_style: Opportunistic early breakaway specialist who capitalizes on terrain advantages, particularly gravel sectors. Shows ability to read race dynamics and exploit when GC riders focus on each other.
team_tactics: Insufficient data to establish clear team tactics patterns. Appears to operate with freedom in breakaways rather than working for protected riders.
terrain_preference: Gravel sectors and technical terrain on home roads (Switzerland). Thrives when course features create tactical complexity that splits focus of stronger teams.
vulnerability: Limited observations suggest vulnerability in pure climbing battles against GC contenders. May lack sprint finish against dedicated sprinters from reduced groups.
key_patterns: Demonstrates opportunistic instincts: takes bonus seconds and exploits breakaway opportunities when available (2 instances)
Performs well on Swiss home roads, showing local knowledge advantage in positioning (2 instances)
Succeeds from early breaks on technical/gravel stages while GC riders neutralize each other (1 definitive win)
form_trajectory: unknown: 2-year gap between observations makes trajectory assessment unreliable, though both show opportunistic capability
